event.name: Mid-Autumn Festival 2026
event.dates: Fri Sep 25 (4–8 PM) + Sat Sep 26 (11 AM–8 PM)
event.tagline: free admission · Friday night market · Saturday festival

hero.headline: Run one room of the Mid-Autumn party
hero.intro: Mid-Autumn Festival returns Sep 25–26 at Derr-Okamoto Park — free admission, Friday night market, Saturday festival, lantern procession at dusk.
hero.subhead: Real families, real constraints — pick the challenge you want to take on.
hero.body: Co-chairs already hold permits, site, and vendor baseline — and bring fintech-style data and automation thinking plus an open-source ethos to nonprofit festival work: automate what repeats, improve quality, publish playbooks others can fork. Nineteen product lanes are open — each is a live testbed: ship one module, document what worked, and contribute to open best practice on Festival Season Network.

steps: Pick one lane (or two if they're closely related — we'll talk). Director filled? You can still apply to join that committee as a Member.
Apply on Idealist — name your lane, preferred contact method (email, text, or phone), and any events you're already committed to this season.
We'll follow up within a few business days — add you to the staff directory, introduce vendor partners or teammates, and set a first check-in.
apply.idealistFallback: Can't use Idealist? Email midautumn@cpalss.com with the lane name and one sentence on what you want to test.

directorIntro.title: What is a product lane lead?
paragraphs: A director is the go-to person for one festival module. You plan the lane — volunteers, members, vendors, or paid contractors may execute it. You don't have to do every shift or task yourself.

Already covered (director): Festival co-chairs (Thuy Pham, Bao Thien Ngo) · Sponsorship (Tido Hoang, with Vivian Vu) · Marketing (Ellie King) · Honor our Elders (Raul Armenta). Members still welcome on filled lanes.
Nineteen product lanes are open for directors. Member and support roles fill in behind every director — including lanes where the director slot is already taken.
notes: Most lanes are planning now through September; peak workload is the two weeks before and event weekend.
Co-chairs encourage every director to find a counterpart at another festival — same lane, different producer — to bounce ideas and share learnings.
Stage owns tent, sound, and physical stack. Program & Entertainment owns the master schedule and MC. Show directors own content lanes and hand run-of-show up to Program.
Power & Lighting installs generators and light towers; Light Up the Park designs decorative lighting — coordinate on loads before either rigs.

lanes.title: Site & operations
lanes.subtitle: Infrastructure modules
lanes.intro: Physical infrastructure, finance, and day-of execution. Vendor revenue funds this baseline.

roles.title: Finance & Administration Director
roles.test: How can one keep festival money honest through a live event weekend — booth collections, county permit pass-through, and closeout under real pressure?
roles.ship: Budget actuals current · service-vendor bills paid · Eventeny reconciliation and deposit closeout by October
roles.fit: Are detail-oriented and comfortable tracking money in and out

roles.title: Foundation Director
roles.test: How can one build a solid base of operations so every other lane can play?
roles.ship: Insurance, CSD permit, ops tents, and contingency signed off before load-in
roles.fit: Like building the platform others run on — checklists, deadlines, and make-it-official work
roles.note: Bounce house rentals require CGL confirmation (or rider) and vendor COI before go-live.

roles.title: Operations & Logistics Director
roles.test: How can one choreograph everyone so they know how to get to the site and load-in without causing a traffic jam?
roles.ship: Site map, load-in/out plan, and day-of ops radio for Fri market + Sat festival
roles.fit: Love maps, timelines, and where does the truck go?

roles.title: Security Director
roles.test: How can one ensure safety for everyone on a sliding scale of available funds — and plan for contingency conditions?
roles.ship: Guard schedule scaled to budget, contingency plan, and Golden Protection liaison (Fri night + Sat festival hours)
roles.fit: Are calm under pressure; can right-size coverage to the budget, plan for what-if scenarios, and liaise with Golden Protection

roles.title: Amenities Director
roles.test: How can one keep guests comfortable so they can stay as long as they want?
roles.ship: Restrooms, ADA unit, hand-wash placement, and porter service locked before gates open
roles.fit: Think about guest comfort, site flow, and keeping units stocked through Sat close

roles.title: Power & Lighting Director
roles.test: How can one power the park safely — load math, generators, electrical zones, and stage hookups dialed before gates open?
roles.ship: Generators and light towers installed, stage power and electrical zones live — capacity and hookups cleared for Light Up the Park
roles.fit: Understand power math and safety — generators, light towers, load planning, diesel logistics; coordinate with Light Up on decorative loads

lanes.title: Program & experience
lanes.subtitle: What families see and remember
lanes.intro: Stage, schedule, and signature moments — including sponsor-funded features from Build the Festival.

roles.title: Stage Director
roles.experience: Stage tent, portable sound, lion dance
roles.test: How can one ship an MVP stage stack — tent, portable sound, lion dance — on a community budget?
roles.ship: Stage tent, sound, and Saturday programming incl. lion dance
roles.fit: Have production or AV experience (helpful, not required)

roles.title: Program & Entertainment Director
roles.experience: Full Saturday run-of-show — talent, MC, sponsor and host moments, lion dance, and show-director modules slotted into the master clock
roles.test: How can one keep the public entertained while keeping entertainment participants on time?
roles.ship: Saturday run-of-show, MC scripts, and talent cues locked with Stage Director before event week
roles.get: Network with talent — MCs, performers, and family entertainers you'll coordinate all season
roles.fit: Love show flow for a family crowd, building relationships with performers, and keeping talent on schedule without losing the audience — ready to be the programming foundation show directors build on

roles.title: Lantern Procession Director
roles.experience: Children with LED lanterns, music, dusk walk through the park
roles.test: How can one create a moment families — especially children — will remember?
roles.ship: One walk-through on Sat evening with families through the park
roles.fit: Love ritual moments and can stage volunteers without micromanaging

roles.title: Children's Village Director
roles.experience: Shade, crafts, family meetup before the procession — plus bounce houses and inflatable carnival games when sponsors fund those blocks
roles.test: How can one keep children entertained through interaction, rather than walk-and-see — without it turning into chaos?
roles.ship: Children's Village open Sat with anchor hosts and crafts · bounce house rentals (vendor COI + Foundation CGL sign-off) · inflatable carnival games when sponsors fund those blocks
roles.fit: Think like a school picnic organizer — do stations and anchor hosts, not empty tents to walk past

roles.title: Children's Show Director
roles.experience: Short Saturday stage sets — magic, storytelling, puppetry
roles.test: How can one book and stage family sets — magic, storytelling, puppetry — that hold kids' attention in 15–20 minute blocks?
roles.ship: Talent booked and children's sets run-of-show handed to Program & Entertainment Director before event week
roles.fit: Have booked youth or family entertainment before (helpful, not required) — you own a show module; Program owns the master clock

roles.title: Honor our Elders Director
roles.experience: Shade, seating, and tea hospitality for elders — grandparents with families, and anyone who came on their own who deserves a warm welcome
roles.test: How can one offer tea hospitality so every elder feels honored — not only grandparents in groups, but also someone who showed up alone?
roles.ship: Shade tent, seating, and elder tea station staffed and welcoming before Sat peak
roles.fit: Understand hospitality as respect — Mid-Autumn is for honoring elders, whoever walks in
roles.note: Director: Raul Armenta · Members welcome — inquire to join the committee

roles.title: Light Up the Park Director
roles.experience: Dusk atmosphere — tree uplights, string lights, stage flowers
roles.test: How can one design and install the park's dusk atmosphere — uplights, string lights, stage flowers — so the vibes land for procession hour?
roles.ship: Decorative lighting designed, installed, and live for procession hour — loads and hookups coordinated with Power & Lighting
roles.fit: Have an eye for atmosphere and hands-on setup — vibe design and decorative rigging; Power installs generators and light towers

lanes.title: Outreach & partners
lanes.subtitle: Audience and ally modules
lanes.intro: Director filled? Full lane details still apply — inquire to join as a Member.

roles.title: Sponsorship Director
roles.experience: Registry stories funded on Build the Festival — lantern procession, children's programming, Honor our Elders, and site lighting unlock when sponsors claim a block
roles.test: How can one create win-win-win situations — great guest experiences, producers who make it happen, and sponsors growing their business?
roles.ship: Pipeline active, registry asks out, sponsor deliverables tracked through event weekend
roles.get: Business development network — coalition merchants and regional sponsors
roles.fit: Think in win-win-win — guests get memorable experiences, producers get resources to ship them, sponsors get real business growth
roles.note: Director: Tido Hoang · Member: Vivian Vu · Members welcome

roles.title: Marketing & Communications Director
roles.experience: Families find the festival — save-the-dates, registry unlock posts, week-of rally
roles.test: How can one create the most effective approach for getting the word out — and gathering public feedback that feeds into the design of future events?
roles.ship: Email and social live through event week · outreach coordinated with Sponsorship · public feedback captured and routed to lane post-mortems and next-season design
roles.fit: Think in two-way comms — reach families where they are, and listen to what they tell you for the next event

roles.title: Vendor Operations Director
roles.test: How can one create a winning balance of vendor profitability and guest experience — families looking for great tastes?
roles.ship: Eventeny live — applications processed, category caps enforced, county permit pass-through, waitlist through event weekend
roles.fit: Care about food-market economics and guest experience — curated composition, fair process, comfortable saying no when a category is full

roles.title: Volunteers Director
roles.test: How can one create a great experience for volunteers — and help them see the value of their contributions?
roles.ship: Recruitment, shifts, waivers, and appreciation — executed by shift leads and day-of crew, with a contractor-backed fallback plan when critical slots won't fill
roles.fit: Think about volunteer experience and recognition — honest shifts, clear roles, appreciation that shows people their work mattered

roles.title: Partnerships & Grants Director
roles.test: How can one position MAF as a pillar of the community-at-large — and build mutually beneficial relationships with every organization around it?
roles.ship: Community partnership map; CCSD liaison, cross-promo allies (e.g. Giant Pumpkin), grant language and renewals; outreach log separating confirmed partners from exploratory threads; festival design advisory — when a grant opens a door we hadn't considered, rough-shape the idea on the Build the Festival gift registry until a lane director owns and details it
roles.fit: Think in mutual benefit across the local ecosystem — not just sponsorship asks; comfortable building institutional relationships and advising on festival design; can give grant-born ideas basic registry shape before handing off to a lane director

phase2.title: Sponsor unlocks — Phase 2
phase2.intro: These lanes activate when sponsors fund the matching Build the Festival gift. Plan with the base lane lead in the meantime; hold the vision until the block unlocks.
roles: Stage+ Director — How can one upgrade to concert-quality sound and basic stage lights beyond MVP?
Security+ Director — How can one add guards at peak hours when sponsors fund the block?
Amenities+ Director — How can one scale restrooms and sinks for a busy Saturday crowd?
Spread the Word Director — How can one turn sponsor dollars into paid Meta / Instagram reach that fills the park?

faq.a: Festival organizing hits the same failure modes as messy ops — handoffs, one-off spreadsheets, knowledge in people's heads. Co-chairs apply fintech and automation thinking with an open-source ethos: automate what repeats, improve quality, and publish forkable playbooks on Festival Season Network.

faq.q: Should I find a counterpart at another festival?
faq.a: Yes — we encourage it. Every director should network with someone running the same lane at another festival or event. Co-chairs can help make an intro if you don't have one yet.
Helpful, not required. LNY runbooks and co-chairs back you up.

These are volunteer leadership roles — you plan the lane; others may execute. Sponsor-funded experiences may include modest stipends for performers or vendors — not director compensation. Flag contractor needs early if volunteers won't cover the work.
One name on the directory as Director; co-leads listed as Members is fine.

faq.q: What if the director slot is already filled?
faq.a: Apply anyway — name the lane and ask to join as a Member. Every lane can use more members even when the director seat is taken.

faq.q: What if my experience isn't funded yet?
faq.a: Plan anyway — lane leads help pitch sponsors and hold the vision until the block unlocks.

faq.q: What's the difference between site & ops and program?
faq.a: Site & ops keeps the gates open, power on, and guests comfortable. Program & experience is what families come for — stage, procession, children's programming, and elder hospitality.

sections.title: About Mid-Autumn / Trung Thu
paragraphs: Mid-Autumn Festival, known as Tết Trung Thu in Vietnamese culture, is a cherished celebration when families gather under the full moon. Children carry lanterns, share mooncakes, and honor elders — a night of reunion, gratitude, and hope. For Vietnamese communities in America, the festival reconnects generations and keeps cultural traditions alive in a new homeland.

The lantern procession — children walking together at dusk — is the signature moment families remember. MAF 2026 brings that ritual back to Derr-Okamoto Park alongside food vendors, stage programming, and elder hospitality.

sections.title: Elk Grove & Derr-Okamoto Park
paragraphs: Mid-Autumn Festival 2026 is a free, family-centered weekend at Derr-Okamoto Park in Elk Grove: Friday night market (Sep 25, 4–8 PM) and Saturday festival (Sep 26, 11 AM–8 PM) with lantern procession at dusk.

The festival is co-hosted by Vietnamese American Community of Sacramento (VACOS), Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento (cPALSs), Norcal Vietnamese Chamber of Commerce (NVCC), and Cosumnes Community Services District (CCSD), with support from sponsors, donors, and volunteers.
